message:
fix(pager): consolidate page read path and add short-read validation

Three related improvements to the pager and cache subsystem:

1. arc_cache.rs -- deduplicate superseded-version eviction logic:
   The evict_one_preferred() method previously contained inline logic
   to find a superseded victim in T1 or T2, evict it, move its key to
   the corresponding ghost list, and update bookkeeping. This was a
   near-exact copy of logic already encapsulated in the existing
   coalesce_one_superseded_for_replace() method. The inline duplicate
   is replaced with a single call to that method, reducing ~20 lines
   of duplicated code and ensuring future changes to eviction policy
   only need to be made in one place.

2. page_cache.rs -- validate read length in ensure_cached():
   Previously, ensure_cached() discarded the return value of
   file.read() and silently inserted a partially-filled buffer into
   the cache if the read returned fewer bytes than a full page. This
   could cause downstream corruption or panics when the short page was
   later interpreted as a complete SQLite page. Now the byte count is
   checked and a DatabaseCorrupt error is returned for short reads,
   matching the validation that was already present in pager.rs.

3. pager.rs -- simplify read_page_inner() via PageCache delegation:
   The old implementation manually acquired a buffer from the pool,
   performed the file read, validated the read length, copied data
   into a Vec, then separately inserted a fresh buffer into the cache
   and copied the data again. This involved two buffer allocations and
   two copies per cache miss.

   The new implementation delegates to self.cache.read_page() which
   handles the read-and-cache in a single operation. On OutOfMemory,
   it attempts one eviction and retries; if eviction fails, it falls
   back to a direct heap-allocated read (graceful cache degradation).
   This eliminates the double-copy, reduces the code from ~40 lines
   to ~20, and centralizes the read+validate+cache logic in PageCache.

Cargo.lock: bumps fsqlite-vfs from 0.1.1 to 0.1.2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
